title: Phase 3 sidecar — components
description: Structured component catalog the Phase-3 (Architecture Modeling) LLM persists at PHASE_END. Consumed by scripts/build_threat_model_yaml.py to populate threat-model.yaml[components[]] without re-composing from working memory in Phase 11.

schema_version.description: Sidecar schema version. Bump only on breaking changes; add new fields via additions[] entries.

id.description: Canonical component ID used as component_id in threats[] and dispatch-context filenames. Lowercase + hyphens only.

description.description: 1-3 sentence summary of the component's role and technology stack.

paths.description: Glob patterns mapping the component to its source files. Source of truth for the Phase-9 dirty-set mapping — MUST reflect actual directory layout.

tier.description: Three-tier architecture classification used by the heatmap renderer. ≥1 component with tier=data is required when the app uses a database/cache/filestore.

complexity.description: Sizes the STRIDE-analyzer turn budget downstream (MAX_TURNS=22/22/31).

framework.description: Primary framework (express, angular, react, sequelize, fastapi, ...). Optional, surfaces in §2.4 layer tables.

deployment_zones.description: Network/trust placement of this component. Values come from the canonical access-zone vocabulary in data/actors/default-library.yaml (e.g. internet, dmz, client-device, mobile-device, internal-network, peer-service, prod-env, prod-write-db, ci-cd-runtime, build-pipeline, deployment-pipeline). Phase-3-authored: the analyst maps the recon component_hints[].deployment_zones (from .recon-signals.json) onto its own finalized component set, resolving the recon↔analyst ID mismatch by judgment. Consumed by the deterministic STRIDE-component selection predicate (manifest builder) to derive exposure — NOT a hard-coded count. Empty/absent (or only runtime-only tags like docker-container) = treat as exposure-unknown (fail-safe toward inclusion at every depth, including quick).

handles_sensitive_data.description: Crown-jewel flag: true when the component stores or processes credentials, PII, payment data, or secrets. Phase-3-authored from the component's own role (the analyst already knows this while writing the description). Lets the selection predicate keep a high-value datastore in scope even when it is not directly internet-exposed.

additions.description: Optional cross-phase additions discovered after Phase 3 — usually empty. Reserved for future use; current pipeline does not populate.
